scope_declaration.training_distribution: Dreaddit corpus — Reddit stress posts, English, WEIRD population (Western, Educated, Industrialized, Rich, Democratic)
scope_declaration.in_distribution_rationale: Text is first-person stress/overwhelm self-report — structurally similar to Dreaddit training data. Domain label 'clinical' suggests a clinical relationship context; Dreaddit has no clinical relationship. Confidence penalty applied for domain shift.
scope_declaration.scope_boundary: Validated for: social media stress text, English, self-report format. Not validated for: clinical intake notes, therapist-authored text, non-English, non-WEIRD populations.

limitations.description: Confidence outputs are anti-calibrated in v3. All 10 dimensions returned confidence < 0.6 (range: 0.416–0.549). The exclusion threshold (0.6) eliminates all dimensions from the aggregate. PSQ composite score (50/100) is a fallback default — not a scored output. Do not interpret 50/100 as neutral.
limitations.mitigation: Use per-dimension scores directly. The raw scores are informative even when confidence is low; anti-calibration affects the confidence number, not necessarily the score ordering.

limitations.description: Training data is WEIRD (Western, Educated, Industrialized, Rich, Democratic). Constructs such as 'contractual_clarity' and 'authority_dynamics' are culturally situated. Scores on these dimensions have higher uncertainty for non-WEIRD populations.

Energy dissipation dimension shows compression at the low end. Score of 1.65/10 may understate depletion severity. The model was trained on shorter Reddit posts; longer first-person reflections with sustained depletion markers may be under-scored.
PSQ composite (50/100) is not usable for this inference. All dimensions excluded from aggregation due to anti-calibration. Composite score is meaningless — report per-dimension scores only.

psq_composite.status: excluded — fallback default (all dimensions below confidence threshold)

dimensions.interpretation: Strong depletion signal. Lowest score of all dimensions. Highest confidence of threat factors. Text markers: 'piling up', 'don't see a way through', 'doesn't feel that way' — sustained exhaustion pattern.
Moderate. No explicit hostility markers in text. Score may reflect latent frustration ('keep telling myself it will get better but it doesn't feel that way').
Moderate. Work pressure framed as accumulating and inescapable ('piling up', 'don't see a way through'). Not acute threat — chronic pressure pattern.
Moderate. No explicit authority figures named. Score may reflect implicit power asymmetry (work demands framed as external imposition).
Below midpoint. Some regulatory attempt visible ('keep telling myself it will get better') but self-talk framed as ineffective.
Low. Recovery capacity depleted. No evidence of prior successful coping in text.
Low. Absence of trust indicators — no mention of supportive relationships, safety, or predictable environment.
Below midpoint. Lowest confidence of all dimensions. Text provides few signals for de-escalation capacity.
Low. No protective structures visible in text — no named coping strategies, support systems, or boundaries.
Highest protective score. Some clarity about the situation present ('work is piling up' is specific). Low confidence — culturally situated construct.

psq_lite_coverage.note: 3 of 10 dimensions map to PSQ-Lite (obs). The most clinically salient signal (energy_dissipation: 1.65) is NOT in PSQ-Lite.
psq_lite_coverage.information_loss_example: energy_dissipation (1.65, conf 0.549) is the strongest signal in this text. PSQ-Lite would miss it entirely. A triage pass using PSQ-Lite would flag this text as moderate (threat_exposure 4.32) rather than high-concern (energy_dissipation 1.65).

claims.text: All 10 PSQ-Full dimensions scored in one forward pass. Raw scores reflect model output. Confidence values are anti-calibrated — they understate actual model certainty and should not be used as reliability indicators without recalibration.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct ONNX inference. 0.85 not 1.0: known anti-calibration issue means score reliability cannot be fully quantified from model outputs alone.

claims.text: Energy dissipation (1.65/10) is the primary clinical signal in this text. It has the highest confidence of all threat dimensions (0.549) and the lowest score. The text presents a sustained depletion pattern, not an acute crisis.
claims.confidence_basis: Score ordering consistent with text content. Confidence capped at 0.80 due to anti-calibration issue — cannot rule out that another dimension is actually primary.

claims.text: PSQ composite (50/100) is the fallback default — all dimensions excluded. Do not report this score to end users or use in downstream calculations.
claims.confidence_basis: All 10 confidence values below 0.6 threshold; aggregation code returns default 50 when n=0 included dimensions.

schema_gap_observations.note: Item 2a derivation findings — what this response could not express cleanly in v2 schema.

gaps.description: No v2 field distinguishes 'dimension scored but below confidence threshold' from 'dimension not scored'. Added per-dimension meets_threshold field in this response — not in v2 spec.
PSQ composite 50/100 is a fallback default, not a scored value. v2 source_confidence is at message level — no per-output usability flag. Added scores.psq_composite.usable field — not in v2 spec.

Limitations disclosure has no structured severity field in v2. Added severity: HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW per limitation — not in v2 spec. epistemic_flags is a flat string array; can't distinguish model-wide vs. dimension-specific limitations.
No field in v2 marks which scores are available in the PSQ-Lite tier. Added psq_lite_mapped: boolean per dimension — not in v2 spec. Required for the triage integration pattern.

action_gate.gate_condition: Composite score is not usable. Per-dimension scores are available with caveats. Do not use PSQ-Full scores in clinical decision-making without human review.
action_gate.gate_note: Scores are informative for research and derivation purposes. Anti-calibration means confidence values understate reliability — treat all dimensions as provisional.

source.source_confidence_basis: Model is validated (r=0.684 on Dreaddit held-out). Anti-calibration issue and partial domain shift reduce confidence below 1.0.

setl_definition: Low inferential distance. Editorial layer adds interpretation of score patterns; structural layer contains raw model outputs. Interpretations are clearly labeled and capped at 0.80 confidence.
epistemic_flags: PSQ composite 50/100 is a fallback default — all 10 dimensions below confidence threshold. Do not use composite.
Anti-calibration: confidence values (0.416–0.549) understate model reliability. Recalibration via isotonic regression is implemented but not applied here — recalibration data not loaded.
Domain shift: text labeled 'clinical reflection' — Dreaddit training data is social media stress. Partial overlap; confidence penalty applied implicitly via lower model certainty.
WEIRD assumptions apply to culturally situated dimensions (contractual_clarity, authority_dynamics).
energy_dissipation (1.65) is the most salient signal and NOT covered by PSQ-Lite — concrete information loss example for Item 2a derivation.
